title: AlUla Part 2: A Graveyard of Empires
_note: INSTITUTIONAL / lecture-grade style (theme 'atlas': ivory + ink-green + gold). Every beat is an animated slide — annotated map, chronology timeline, drawn-on Nabataean facade, pull-quote, or statement — all with constant motion. NO RECITAL: the Qur'an appears as on-screen Arabic + English only (beat 12); voice speaks English. ACCURACY: the Nabataean facade (beat 10) is shown ONLY for the Nabataeans; Thamud's carving (beat 4) is a statement, never illustrated with the Nabataean tombs. Every beat carries a sourced footnote. TTS: AlUla='al-OO-la', al-Hijr='al-HIJR', Thamud='tha-MOOD', Dadan='da-DAN', Lihyan='LIH-yan', Nabataeans='nab-a-TEE-ans'. For #HistoryTikTokContest (1min+).

segments.foot: Hegra (Madāʾin Ṣāliḥ) & AlUla, the Hejaz, Saudi Arabia
segments.text: This valley in Saudi Arabia hides a secret almost no tourist is ever told.

segments.foot: Sources: UNESCO; Hoyland, Arabia and the Arabs
segments.text: It isn't one lost city. It's four, stacked on the very same sand.
And the Quran named the most terrifying of them first.

segments.foot: Qur'an 15:82 — 'they carved homes from the mountains, feeling secure'
segments.text: Thamud carved their homes into solid mountains, then defied their own prophet.

segments.foot: Qur'an 15:83 — 'the shriek seized them at morning'
segments.text: He warned them. They refused. And a single blast left their cliffs empty forever.

segments.foot: After: Healey, The Nabataean Tomb Inscriptions of Madāʾin Ṣāliḥ
segments.text: So why did empire after empire bleed for this one patch of desert?

segments.foot: The caravan incense route, South Arabia → the Mediterranean
segments.text: It sat on the incense road, the richest trade route in the ancient world. Whoever held the oasis taxed it all.
First rose the kingdom of Dadan, already powerful nearly three thousand years ago.

segments.foot: Colossal Lihyanite statues, excavated at Dadan
segments.text: Then mighty Lihyan, whose giant stone statues are still being pulled from the sand.

segments.foot: Hegra: 100+ monumental rock-cut tombs · 1st c. BCE – 1st c. CE
segments.text: Then the Nabataeans, the builders of Petra, who carved the tombs every tourist photographs today.
Four powers. One valley. Each certain it could never fall. Every one now silent stone.
And the Quran tells us exactly what to do with a place like this. Travel the earth, and see how the story of those before you ended.
AlUla was never a luxury escape. It is a graveyard of empires.
A sign, the Quran says, left standing for anyone willing to reflect.

sources: Qur'an, Surah Al-Hijr 15:80-84 — Thamud of al-Hijr; 15:82 they carved homes in the mountains; 15:83 the shriek (sayhah). Also Surah Al-A'raf 7:73-79.
Dadan (Dedan): oasis kingdom at al-Khuraybah, AlUla; early-mid 1st millennium BCE.
Lihyan (Lihyanite kingdom): c. 5th-2nd c. BCE; monumental Lihyanite statues excavated at Dadan.

Nabataeans (1st c. BCE - 1st c. CE): builders of Petra; carved the monumental tombs of Hegra (Madāʾin Ṣāliḥ). The facade shown belongs ONLY to the Nabataean beat; Thamud's carving (beat 4) is a statement, never the Nabataean tombs.
Incense road: AlUla/Dadan was a key caravan station carrying frankincense and myrrh north from southern Arabia.
Qur'an, Surah Ar-Rum 30:42 (cf. 27:69) — 'Travel through the land and observe how was the end of those before' (shown as Arabic + English; NOT recited).

ACCURACY: dating of Qur'anic Thamud is debated; the chronology presents the dated kingdoms alongside the Qur'anic account without forcing a strict order. The false 'ozone hole over AlUla' claim is excluded.
